title: clinopodium ashei
intro: Clinopodium ashei, also known as Ashe's Clinopodium, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States, particularly in the states of Alabama and Georgia. Clinopodium ashei has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to have various health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing stress, and promoting respiratory health.
